title: ORCA Hook Output
description: JSON output format for orca when used as a Claude Code PreToolUse hook. Emitted when a command is denied or when policy requests an ask/warn decision; allowed commands produce no output.

hookSpecificOutput.description: The hook-specific output payload for Claude Code

hookEventName.description: The hook event type, always 'PreToolUse' for orca

permissionDecision.description: The permission decision: 'deny' blocks the command, 'ask' asks/warns before continuing, and 'allow' permits it

permissionDecisionReason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the command was blocked, including the rule ID, reason, and remediation instructions

allowOnceCode.description: Short numeric code for one-time bypass via 'orca allow-once <code>'

allowOnceFullHash.description: Full SHA-256 hash of the command for verification

ruleId.description: Stable pattern identifier in format 'pack.category:pattern-name' for allowlisting

packId.description: The security pack that matched, in format 'category.name'

severity.description: Severity level of the blocked command

remediation.description: Suggested remediation for the blocked command

safeAlternative.description: A safer alternative command that achieves similar results

explanation.description: Explanation of why the alternative is safer or what the user should do

allowOnceCommand.description: The full 'orca allow-once <code>' command for one-time bypass

hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecisionReason:
BLOCKED by orca

Tip: orca explain "git reset --hard HEAD~5"

Reason: git reset --hard destroys uncommitted changes

Rule: core.git:reset-hard

Command: git reset --hard HEAD~5

remediation.explanation: Use git stash to save your changes before resetting.
